title: salix pedicellata subsp. canariensis
intro: Salix pedicellata subsp. canariensis, commonly known as Canary willow or Canary willow tree, is a subspecies of willow tree native to the Canary Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Salix pedicellata subsp. canariensis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in wound healing, as well as having potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects.
